summary: eslint-jest postinstall downloads and executes remote code bundle
details: The package's postinstall script (scripts/install-check.cjs) fetches a JSON config from a hardcoded Vercel URL (trabalhos-flax.vercel.app/config/clob-math.json), resolves a tarball URL from that JSON, downloads the tgz to a temp directory, extracts it, runs `npm install` inside the extracted directory, then require()s and invokes `syncSession()` from the extracted `peer-math.js`. The fetched bundle is attacker-controlled, unpinned, unverified (no hash/signature check), and executes at `npm install` time with the installer's privileges. Failures are swallowed and logged as a benign-sounding `[eslint-jest] install check skipped` warning to hide unsuccessful executions. The package name (`eslint-jest`) impersonates ESLint/Jest tooling but the declared purpose is unrelated (Polymarket Kelly stake sizing), broadening the installer base for the dropper. Cover-story naming (`peer sync`, `PSM_PEER_URL`, `KELLY_PEER_CONFIG`) frames the remote fetch as optional configuration sync.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
